codeReview.summary: The feature adds substantial native-auth coverage tests, but the underlying native token verification wiring is not safe/complete: relay verification defaults to an empty signing key while CLI login signs with a per-device random key, creating both a fail-open security posture and a client/relay key mismatch path.

issues.description: Relay token verification defaults to an empty signing key (`process.env['MORS_RELAY_SIGNING_KEY'] ?? ''`). This violates fail-closed auth expectations: if unset, tokens signed with the known empty key can be forged and accepted instead of producing an actionable prerequisite failure.

`mors login` signs session tokens with a local randomly generated signing key (`loadSigningKey`/`generateSigningKey`/`saveSigningKey`), but relay verification uses `MORS_RELAY_SIGNING_KEY` from server env. There is no key coordination path or explicit remediation when these differ, so login-issued tokens are not reliably verifiable by the relay.
CLI help text still says `login` authenticates with GitHub OAuth device flow, which conflicts with the hard-switch to mors-native auth and can mislead users during onboarding.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture notes are stale for identity/auth and still describe GitHub OAuth device flow, despite mission and feature requirements being hard-switched to mors-native auth.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:13 says 'Identity/auth via GitHub OAuth Device Flow'; mission AGENTS requires native auth at /Users/justen/.factory/missions/54bd84c5-2680-4f00-890c-9c832860b3d4/AGENTS.md:22

sharedStateObservations.observation: Environment documentation does not document relay signing-key requirements, which is now central to native token verification behavior and troubleshooting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/environment.md:21 still lists GitHub device-flow vars; .env.example includes GITHUB_* placeholders (line 5) and no MORS_RELAY_SIGNING_KEY

summary: Reviewed feature native-auth-invite-session-core using feature metadata, handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, and skill guidance. Although validation tests were added successfully, blocking auth-key wiring flaws remain in production token verification/login issuance, so the feature does not yet safely satisfy the native auth hard-switch expectations.
